description: Deliberately ambiguous signals. One PR has been open 5 days but has ongoing discussion, not neglect. One engineer asked two questions about a topic but also shipped a PR on it, so 'struggling with X' would be a false read. The model should resist reading morale into this week.

repo:acme/platform.content:
PR #901: Migrate metrics pipeline from statsd to OpenTelemetry

Larger RFC-style PR. Replaces the in-house statsd client with the OTel SDK. Includes a migration guide for service owners and a compatibility shim for the next two quarters.
Review on #901 (commented): Big PR but well-organized. I left notes on the shim — I think we can simplify the tag-rewriting by using a resource detector instead. Would love your take.
Review on #901 (commented): Agreed with Priya on the shim simplification. Also: how does this interact with the sampling config in config/otel.yaml? I couldn't tell from the PR description.
Review on #901 (commented): Good point, Diego. Sampling is applied at the exporter layer, so the shim doesn't see it. I updated the PR description and added a section to the migration guide.
Review on #901 (approved): The resource-detector change looks great. Merging hold for Diego's ack on the sampling thread.

PR #905: Wire up OTel tracing in the auth service

First service to adopt the new OTel pipeline from #901. Adds 3 spans around the login path and verifies traces show up in the dev dashboard.

slack:eng-platform.content: #eng-platform: dumb question — when you add an OTel span, does it automatically get the service.name resource attribute or do you need to set it yourself?
#eng-platform (thread): Not dumb. It's auto-populated if the SDK's resource detector runs, which is the default in the new pipeline. You only need to set it manually if you're using the raw exporter.
#eng-platform: follow-up: if I want to add a custom attribute to every span from my service, is that a processor or a resource? I keep getting confused about the difference.

#eng-platform (thread): Resources are per-service and static. Processors run per-span and can mutate. For 'custom attribute on every span' it could be either — resource is cheaper if the value doesn't change at runtime.
#eng-platform: ok, going with resource. Thanks. PR #905 is up for review whenever anyone has cycles.
